pattern: A 10-bit parity mask of letter counts reduces the at-most-one-odd condition to counting prefix masks that match or differ in a single bit.
hints: Only the parity of each letter's count matters, and there are just ten letters, so encode the running parity as a 10-bit mask.
A substring is wonderful when the XOR of its two endpoint prefix masks has at most one set bit.
Count occurrences of each prefix mask; for every new prefix add the count of the equal mask plus the counts of masks differing by exactly one bit.

edge_inputs.description: uses the full a-j alphabet once each

stress.description: long string over the a-j alphabet
short string for differential oracle agreement
